# Jeremie Louvaert — Full Context for LLMs

> Open-source developer specializing in ComfyUI custom node development. Founder of AKURATE.STUDIO, a premium brand intelligence platform. Based in Europe, building tools at the intersection of generative AI and production workflows since 2012 (GitHub member).

## About

Jeremie Louvaert builds production-grade open-source tooling for the ComfyUI ecosystem. ComfyUI is the leading node-based interface for running Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Flux, and other AI image/video generation models. His work focuses on solving practical problems that arise when moving from local GPU compute to cloud API-based workflows, and on bringing conversational AI capabilities into node-based image editing.

## Project: ComfyUI-Gemini-Conversation-Canvas

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-Gemini-Conversation-Canvas
Language: Python
License: MIT

### Problem Statement
Traditional image generation APIs (DALL-E, Midjourney, Flux) are single-shot: send a prompt, get an image, no memory between generations. Google Gemini's native image generation is different — it maintains conversation context, remembering what it generated and reasoning about modifications. However, no ComfyUI node suite properly exploited this multi-turn capability.

### Solution
Five custom nodes that bring Gemini's conversational image editing into ComfyUI:

1. Gemini Session (Start)
   - Creates a new conversation and generates the initial image
   - Supports text-to-image and image-to-image editing
   - Configurable model selection and aspect ratio

2. Gemini Edit Turn
   - Sends a natural language edit instruction to an existing conversation
   - Full scene coherence maintained across turns
   - Chainable — connect multiple in sequence for multi-step edits in one run
   - Optional reference image input per turn

3. Gemini Session (Save)
   - Persists conversation history and turn images to disk
   - API keys excluded from saved data for security
   - Concurrent-safe with FileLock + atomic writes

4. Gemini Session (Load)
   - Restores saved sessions for cross-run continuation
   - Rebuilds Gemini Chat object from serialized history with thought signature preservation

5. Gemini Session Gallery
   - Outputs all turn images as a batch tensor for visual comparison
   - Includes turn log with response text summaries

### Architecture
Session state flows between nodes as a Python dict containing serialized Gemini Content objects (via Pydantic model_dump). Images saved as separate PNGs to keep session JSON lightweight. Chat objects rebuilt from history using google-genai SDK's chats.create(history=...) pattern.

### Key Technical Features
- Thought signature preservation for Gemini 3.x model compatibility
- History serialization/deserialization via Pydantic model_validate
- Concurrent-safe file operations with FileLock + atomic writes (temp file + os.replace)
- API key resolution: direct input > GEMINI_API_KEY env var > gemini_api_key.txt file
- ComfyUI IMAGE tensor ↔ PIL Image ↔ bytes conversion utilities

## Project: ComfyUI-API-Optimizer

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-API-Optimizer
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
When ComfyUI workflows shift from local GPU inference to cloud APIs (Kling 3.0, Magnific, Banana.dev, RunPod), the bottlenecks change from VRAM to API costs, latency, and serialization. ComfyUI's native caching often breaks with external API nodes due to dynamic timestamps and non-deterministic seeds.

### Solution
Three custom node types that integrate natively into ComfyUI:

1. API Cost & Quota Tracker
   - Circuit-breaker pattern for API spending
   - Persistent JSON ledger with per-provider cost tracking
   - Append-only JSONL transaction audit log with timestamps
   - Budget enforcement that halts execution before API charges
   - Uses decimal.Decimal for precise arithmetic (no floating-point drift)
   - Concurrent-safe with file locking

2. Deterministic Hash Vault (3 nodes)
   - Hash Vault Check: Hashes prompt text, parameters, and full tensor byte content
   - Lazy API Switch: Uses ComfyUI's lazy evaluation to physically prevent upstream API node execution on cache hits
   - Hash Vault Save: Writes API outputs to disk cache with optional TTL
   - Features: recursive hashing for nested structures, device-portable caching, atomic writes

## Project: comfyui-prompt-assembler

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/comfyui-prompt-assembler
Language: Python
License: MIT

### Purpose
Assemble prompts from multiple string inputs with per-slot ON/OFF toggles. Three variants: standard 8-slot, compact 4-slot, and weighted 6-slot with attention syntax support.

## Project: comfyui-wireless-link-simple

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/comfyui-wireless-link-simple
Language: Python
License: Free to use and modify

### Purpose
Minimal wireless data link implementation for ComfyUI, inspired by Blackmagic Fusion's wireless links. Two nodes (Send/Get) with named channels and universal type support.
